close_reason: Not a finding: all async backends require a runtime, sign_sync adapter exists in secretx-core
description: The PKCS#11 backend can be used in both async and sync contexts because it implements the same trait pattern. Tpm2Backend::sign(), get(), put(), and public_key_der() all call tokio::task::spawn_blocking unconditionally. Unlike PKCS#11 (which opens sessions synchronously in from_uri), the TPM2 backend has no sync fallback — it always requires a tokio runtime. If secretx-core provides a sign_sync() adapter (as hinted by the git log commit 'refactor: take &dyn SigningBackend in sign_sync'), callers using that adapter in a non-tokio context will panic at the spawn_blocking call. The trait methods are async, so this is arguably correct, but it differs from PKCS#11's behavior where from_uri() itself does blocking work. This is a design divergence worth documenting: callers must have a tokio runtime active to use any Tpm2Backend operation.
title: No sign_sync implementation; Tpm2Backend requires tokio runtime
